prompt: Load 'default.s2p' and convert the S-parameters to both Z-parameters and Y-parameters using the object-based approach. Print the impedance at 2 GHz for Z11 and the admittance for Y11. Verify round-trip accuracy by converting Z back to S and computing the maximum error.
expected_output: Code using zparameters(s) and yparameters(s) for object-based conversion, rfparam to extract Z11 and Y11, and sparameters(z) for round-trip with error check.

prompt: Load 'default.s4p' and compute the differential-mode S-parameters (Sdd) and the cross-mode S-parameters (Sdc). Print the size of each result. Use default port ordering (option 1).
expected_output: Code using s2sdd and s2sdc on the 4-port Parameters array to produce 2x2xK differential and cross-mode results.

prompt: Load 'default.s2p', convert S to Z parameters, then convert Z back to S. Compute the maximum absolute error between the original and round-tripped S-parameters. Assert the error is below 1e-10.
expected_output: Code that performs S->Z->S round-trip using object constructors and verifies error < 1e-10.

prompt: Given a 4-port S-parameter file 'default.s4p', extract ports 1 and 3 into a 2-port network, terminating the unused ports with 50 Ohm. Use snp2smp with the correct argument order.
expected_output: Code using snp2smp(s4, [1 3], 50) with port list BEFORE impedance. Must NOT use snp2smp(s4, 50, [1 3]).

prompt: Load 'default.s2p' and convert to both H-parameters and G-parameters. Print the number of ports for each. These conversions only work for 2-port networks.
expected_output: Code using hparameters(s) and gparameters(s) on a 2-port network, confirming NumPorts is 2.

prompt: I have a 2-port device measured in 'default.s2p'. I want to see the input impedance (Z11) as a function of frequency. Convert S to Z-parameters, extract Z11 with rfparam, and plot the real part (resistance) and imaginary part (reactance) on a two-panel figure with proper axis labels including units.
expected_output: Code using zparameters(s) for conversion, rfparam(z,1,1) for extraction, real() and imag() for components, tiledlayout with nexttile for two panels, labeled with Ohm and GHz.
